title: D 2023 026
full_text:
Executive Order D 2023 026 
December 15, 2023 
Page 1 of 2 
D 2023 026 
EXECUTIVE ORDER 
Proclamation 
Declaration of Vote on Proposition II -  
Tobacco and Nicotine Product Tax Revenue Measure 
Pursuant to the authority vested in the Governor of the State of Colorado, and, in 
particular, pursuant to Article V, Section 1(4)(a) of the Colorado Constitution, I, Jared Polis, 
Governor of the State of Colorado, hereby issue this proclamation declaring the vote on 
Proposition II. 
I. 
Background and Purpose 
Proposition II was referred to the people of the state at the coordinated election held on 
November 7, 2023. Secretary of State Jena Griswold certified on December 4, 2023 that she had 
canvassed the “Abstract of Votes Cast,” and that the measure was approved by a majority of the 
votes cast.  
Pursuant to Article V, Section 1(4)(a) of the Colorado Constitution, measures initiated or 
referred to the people of the state shall become law when approved by a majority of the votes 
cast, and shall take effect from and after the date of the official declaration of the vote by 
proclamation of the governor, but not later than thirty days after the vote has been canvassed. 
II. 
Declaration  
Accordingly, I hereby issue this proclamation declaring the vote of the following measure 
approved by the people on November 7, 2023:  
Without raising taxes, may the state retain and spend revenues from taxes on cigarettes, 
tobacco, and other nicotine products and maintain tax rates on cigarettes, tobacco, and 
other nicotine products and use these revenues to invest twenty-three million six hundred 
fifty thousand dollars to enhance the voluntary Colorado preschool program and make it 
widely available for free instead of reducing these tax rates and refunding revenues to 
cigarette wholesalers, tobacco product distributors, nicotine products distributors, and 

Executive Order D 2023 026 
December 15, 2023 
Page 2 of 2  
other taxpayers, for exceeding an estimate included in the ballot information booklet for 
proposition EE? 
Yes - 1,130,047 (67.53 percent) 
No - 543,405 (32.47 percent) 
  
GIVEN under my hand 
and the Executive Seal of the 
State of Colorado, this fifteenth  
day of December 2023. 
Jared Polis 
Governor

title: D 2024 001
full_text:
Executive Order D 2024 001 
January 24, 2024 
Page 1 of 2 
 
D 2024 001 
EXECUTIVE ORDER 
Directing the Development and Prioritization of Plans for 
Commemorating America’s 250th and Colorado’s 150th Anniversaries 
Pursuant to the authority vested in the Governor of the State of Colorado and, in 
particular, pursuant to Article IV, Section 2 of the Colorado Constitution, I, Jared Polis, 
Governor of the State of Colorado, hereby issue this Executive Order directing executive branch 
agencies to develop and prioritize planning for the 250th anniversary of the founding of the 
United States of America and the 150th anniversary of Colorado’s statehood. 
 
Background and Purpose 
Colorado earned the nickname, “the Centennial State,” after joining the United States in 
1876 as the country celebrated the 100th anniversary of the Declaration of Independence. Our 
State is known for many things: near constant sunshine, otherworldly landscape, world-class 
sports, booming economic opportunity, the outdoor economy, and diverse agricultural 
enterprises. And while Colorado is known for its enviable attractions, there exists an 
immeasurable, independent spirit within this State’s history that truly defines what it means to be 
a Coloradan. Our State was the first to support women’s suffrage by popular referendum and the 
first state to legalize recreational use of marijuana, contributing to a Colorado for All. Colorado’s 
future is bright like our famous Rocky Mountain sunshine. 
In 2026, the United States will celebrate its 250th birthday and the Centennial State will 
celebrate its 150th birthday. The overlap of these two momentous anniversaries is an opportunity 
for Colorado to shine a spotlight on our State’s rich history and bright future. This Executive 
Order will further our State’s work to honor Colorado’s statehood and the founding of our 
nation. 
In 2022, I signed SB 22-011, establishing the “America 250-Colorado 150 Commission” 
(Commission). The Commission is tasked with developing programs and planning for the 
official observance of the Semiquincentennial anniversary of the United States and the 
Sesquicentennial anniversary of Colorado’s statehood. Because the Commission is served by 
volunteers, the cooperation and support of State executive branch agencies, community partners, 
and Tribal Nations will be paramount to the success of these celebrations. By directing all State 

Executive Order D 2024 001 
January 24, 2024 
Page 2 of 2 
 
agencies and departments managed by governor-appointed executives (Agencies) to prioritize 
this celebration, we can live up to our legacy of being collaborative, bold, and inclusive. 
 
Declarations and Directives 
A. 
I direct all Agencies to develop and implement plans that incorporate the 
Commission’s goals in achieving a celebration that honors our State and its role in 
history. Agencies are encouraged to align with the Commission’s signature 
initiatives in developing plans for engagement. All Agencies may rely on existing 
programs to support the goals of the Commission. These plans shall address how 
each Agency can contribute to the 250-150 celebration in 2026. All plans should 
also seek to incorporate the 250-150 logo where appropriate.   
B. 
I direct all Agencies to designate staff by April 1, 2024, within each office to 
serve as the liaison for the ongoing work of the Commission. 
C. 
I direct all Agencies to send their plans to the Commission by July 1, 2024. 
 
Duration 
This Executive Order shall expire on December 31, 2026, unless extended further by 
Executive Order of the Governor. 
 
 
GIVEN under my hand and the 
Executive Seal of the State of 
Colorado this twenty-fourth  
day of January, 2024. 
 
 
 
 
Jared Polis 
Governor

title: D 2024 002
full_text:
Executive Order D 2024 002 
February 9, 2024 
Page 1 of 3 
D 2024 002 
EXECUTIVE ORDER 
Declaring a Disaster Emergency Due to Extreme Cold Temperatures Throughout Colorado 
and Authorizing Deployment of the Colorado National Guard 
Pursuant to the authority vested in the Governor of the State of Colorado and, in 
particular, pursuant to Article IV, Section 2, of the Colorado Constitution and the relevant 
portions of the Colorado Disaster Emergency Act, C.R.S. § 24-33.5-701, et seq., I, Jared Polis, 
Governor of the State of Colorado, issue this Executive Order declaring a state of disaster 
emergency due to extreme cold temperatures and winter weather throughout Colorado, enabling 
State and federal agencies to coordinate response, consequence management, hazard mitigation, 
and recovery efforts. 
Further, pursuant to Article IV, Section 5 of the Colorado Constitution and C.R.S. § 28-3-
104, I authorize employing unarmed members of the Colorado National Guard for the purpose of 
responding to and providing support during the extreme cold temperatures and winter weather 
throughout Colorado. 
Background and Purpose 
The Governor is responsible for meeting the dangers to the State and people presented by 
disasters. C.R.S. § 24-33.5-704(1). The Colorado Disaster Emergency Act defines a disaster as 
“the occurrence or imminent threat of widespread or severe damages, injury or loss of life or 
property resulting from any natural cause or cause of human origin.” C.R.S. § 24-33.5-703(3). 
On January 9, 2024, and subsequent days, the National Weather Service predicted an 
arctic blast that would impact Colorado beginning on or before January 12, 2024, and continuing 
through at least January 15, 2024, with a dangerous mix of sub-zero temperatures, strong wind, 
extreme wind chills, and areas of snow. With these dangerous conditions, children, the elderly, 
people with disabilities, people with special medical needs, and people experiencing 
homelessness are especially vulnerable.  
On January 11, 2024, at approximately 5:20 PM, I verbally declared a state of disaster 
emergency due to extreme cold temperatures and winter weather throughout Colorado, and 
authorized employing the Colorado National Guard in an unarmed capacity for the purpose of 
responding to and providing support during the extreme cold temperatures and winter weather 
throughout Colorado.  

Executive Order D 2024 002 
February 9, 2024 
Page 2 of 3 
Beginning on January 11, 2024, and continuing through January 13, 2024, communities 
requested State support to augment staffing for shelters and to provide transportation for people 
experiencing homelessness to shelters. During this period, the State Emergency Operations 
Center provided 35 shelter staff and supported six cold weather transportation missions. The 
National Guard members were on standby to provide shelter staffing support when needed. 
Communities across Colorado were proactively contacted to ensure anticipated needs were met 
and minimize the risk to life. Voluntary agencies provided support in their communities to 
people with identified needs.  
Declarations and Directives 
A.
The extreme cold temperatures and winter weather throughout Colorado and 
associated impacts constitute a disaster emergency under C.R.S. § 24-33.5-701, et 
seq. My verbal order of January 11, 2024, declaring a disaster emergency, is 
hereby memorialized by this Executive Order and shall have the full force and 
effect of law as if it were contained in this Executive Order.
B.
Further, pursuant to C.R.S. § 28-3-104, my verbal order of January 11, 2024, 
authorizing employment and use of the Colorado National Guard in an unarmed 
capacity for the purpose of responding to and providing support during the 
extreme cold temperatures and winter weather throughout Colorado, is hereby 
memorialized by this Executive Order and shall have the full force and effect of 
law as if it were contained in this Executive Order.
C.
My verbal order of January 11, 2024, activated the State Emergency Operations 
Plan. All State departments and agencies were authorized to take whatever actions 
may be required and requested by the Director or their delegate of the Office of 
Emergency Management, pursuant to the State Emergency Operations Plan, 
including provision of appropriate staff and equipment as necessary.
D.
Pursuant to C.R.S. § 24-33.5-706(4)(b), funds in the Disaster Emergency Fund
(DEF) are insufficient to pay for response, consequence management, hazard 
mitigation, and recovery efforts related to the extreme cold temperatures and 
winter weather throughout Colorado. Therefore, I order that one hundred fifty 
thousand dollars ($150,000) be transferred from the State Emergency Reserve 
Cash Fund (SERCF) to the DEF. I encumber one hundred fifty thousand dollars 
($150,000) in the DEF to pay for response, consequence management, hazard 
mitigation, and recovery efforts related to the extreme cold temperatures and 
winter weather throughout Colorado. 

Executive Order D 2024 002 
February 9, 2024 
Page 3 of 3 
E.
Pursuant to C.R.S. § 24-33.5-705.4(5), the Director of the Office of Emergency
Management is hereby authorized and directed to allocate the funding to the
appropriate government agencies and nonprofit organizations and execute awards,
purchase orders, or other mechanisms to effect the allocation of funds. The funds
described in Section II(D) above shall remain available for this purpose for
seventy-two (72) months from the date of this Executive Order, and any
unexpended funds shall remain in the DEF.
Duration 
This Executive Order shall expire thirty (30) days from January 11, 2024, unless 
extended further by Executive Order, except that the funds described in Section II(D) above shall 
remain available for the described purposes for seventy-two (72) months from the date of this 
Executive Order. 
GIVEN under my hand and the 
Executive Seal of the State of 
Colorado this ninth  
day of February, 2024. 
Jared Polis 
Governor

title: D 2024 003
full_text:
Executive Order D 2024 003 
February 9, 2024 
Page 1 of 2 
D 2024 003 
EXECUTIVE ORDER 
Amending Executive Order D 2023 022 Declaring a Disaster Emergency Due to the 
Hazardous Materials Incident in Fremont County 
Pursuant to the authority vested in the Governor of the State of Colorado and, in 
particular, pursuant to Article IV, Section 2, of the Colorado Constitution and the relevant 
portions of the Colorado Disaster Emergency Act, C.R.S. § 24-33.5-701, et seq., I, Jared Polis, 
Governor of the State of Colorado, hereby issue this Executive Order amending Executive Order 
D 2023 022 concerning funding for the disaster emergency due to the Hazardous Materials 
Incident in Fremont County. 
Background and Purpose 
The Governor is responsible for meeting the dangers to the State and people presented by 
disasters. C.R.S. § 24-33.5-704(1). The Colorado Disaster Emergency Act defines a disaster as 
“the occurrence or imminent threat of widespread or severe damages, injury or loss of life or 
property resulting from any natural cause or cause of human origin.” C.R.S. § 24-33.5-703(3). 
On October 27, 2023, I issued Executive Order D 2023 022, which memorialized my 
verbal executive order from October 5, 2023, declaring a state of disaster emergency due to the 
Hazardous Materials Incident in Fremont County. The Executive Order directed State and 
federal agencies to coordinate response, consequence management, hazard mitigation, and 
recovery efforts, and allocated one million dollars ($1,000,000) for this incident. 
During October 2023, 198 deceased individuals were transported from the Fremont 
County incident site to other locations to identify the deceased individuals and return them to 
their families for interment. 
In January 2024, it was determined that 44 individuals required DNA testing to establish 
identification. It was further determined that the cost of DNA testing requires additional funding 
beyond the initial one million dollars ($1,000,000) allocated. An additional two hundred twenty 
thousand dollars ($220,000) is needed to complete the identification process. 
This Executive Order allocates an additional two hundred twenty thousand dollars 
($220,000) for response, consequence management, hazard mitigation, and recovery efforts 
related to the Hazardous Materials Incident. 

Executive Order D 2024 003 
February 9, 2024 
Page 2 of 2 
Amendment 
Section II.E of Executive Order D 2023 022 (Hazardous Materials Incident in Fremont 
County) is amended to read as follows: 
Pursuant to C.R.S. § 24-33.5-706(4)(b), funds in the Disaster Emergency Fund (DEF) are 
insufficient to pay for response, consequence management, hazard mitigation, and 
recovery efforts related to the Hazardous Materials Incident in Fremont County. 
Therefore, I order that one million two hundred twenty thousand dollars ($1,220,000) be 
transferred from the State Emergency Reserve Cash Fund (SERCF) to the DEF. I 
encumber one million two hundred twenty thousand dollars ($1,220,000) in the DEF to 
pay for response, consequence management, hazard mitigation, and recovery efforts 
related to the Hazardous Materials Incident in Fremont County. 
Duration 
This Executive Order shall remain in effect unless modified or rescinded by Executive 
Order. In all other respects, Executive Order D 2023 022 shall remain in full force and effect as 
originally promulgated. 
GIVEN under my hand and the 
Executive Seal of the State of 
Colorado this ninth  
day of February, 2024. 
Jared Polis 
Governor

title: D 2024 004
full_text:
Executive Order D 2024 004 
February 27, 2024 
Page 1 of 2 
 
D 2024 004 
EXECUTIVE ORDER 
Activating the Colorado National Guard to Assist with  
Election Cybersecurity Defense Efforts 
Pursuant to the authority vested in the Governor of the State of Colorado and, in 
particular, pursuant to Article IV, Section 5 of the Colorado Constitution and C.R.S. § 28-3-104, 
et seq., I, Jared Polis, Governor of the State of Colorado, hereby issue this Executive Order 
activating no less than six (6) and up to nine (9) unarmed members of the Colorado National 
Guard Defensive Cyber Operations Element to assist with election cybersecurity defense efforts 
during the 2024 Presidential Primary Election. 
 
Background and Purpose 
Like many states, Colorado uses online voter registration systems that allow eligible 
citizens to access and update their voter registration information. These systems are protected by 
various security features that maintain confidentiality and prevent data tampering or interference 
with data. 
Even with such security features, online voter registration systems could provide an 
avenue for cyber actors to gain unlawful access to voter registration databases. Outside of 
Colorado, voter registration databases have been compromised by cyber actors hacking into 
various systems. While cyber actors are unable to modify voter records, breaches could result in 
the release of voters’ personally identifiable information. The exposure of voters’ personally 
identifiable information does not threaten the integrity of our State elections, but could 
undermine public confidence in the system and suppress voter registration. The Colorado 
National Guard Defensive Cyber Operations Element has unique cyber capabilities to assist in 
protecting critical election infrastructure. 
The Colorado Secretary of State has requested that the Colorado National Guard 
Defensive Cyber Operations Element provide election cybersecurity support on February 27, 
2024, and extending for up to thirty (30) days, for the 2024 Presidential Primary Election. By 
activating no less than six (6) and up to nine (9) unarmed members of the Colorado National 
Guard Defensive Cyber Operations Element, the National Guard will support the Secretary of 
State with election defense efforts up to and including the 2024 Presidential Primary Election on 
March 5, 2024. 

Executive Order D 2024 004 
February 27, 2024 
Page 2 of 2 
 
 
Declarations and Directives 
On January 30, 2024, the Colorado Secretary of State requested support from the 
Colorado National Guard Defensive Cyber Operations Element for the 2024 Presidential Primary 
Election occurring on March 5, 2024. Pursuant to C.R.S. § 28-3-104, the Governor may employ 
the National Guard for the defense of the State. I hereby activate no less than six (6) and up to 
nine (9) unarmed members of the Colorado National Guard Defensive Cyber Operations Element 
to participate in training activities and assist with election cybersecurity defense efforts on 
February 27, 2024, and extending for up to thirty (30) days, for the 2024 Presidential Primary 
Election. 
 
Duration 
This Executive Order shall expire thirty (30) days from February 27, 2024, unless 
extended further by Executive Order. 
 
 
GIVEN under my hand and the 
Executive Seal of the State of 
Colorado this twenty-seventh  
day of February, 2024.  
 
overnor 
Jared Polis 
G

title: D 2024 005
full_text:
Executive Order D 2024 005 
March 23, 2024 
Page 1 of 2 
D 2024 005 
EXECUTIVE ORDER 
Ordering a Congressional Vacancy Election in the Fourth Congressional District of 
Colorado 
Pursuant to the authority vested in the Governor of the State of Colorado and, in 
particular, pursuant to Article I, Section 2, Clause 4, of the United States Constitution, Article 
IV, Section 2 of the Colorado Constitution, and the relevant portions of Colorado law governing 
congressional vacancy elections, C.R.S. § 1-4-401, et seq., I, Jared Polis, Governor of the State 
of Colorado, hereby issue this Executive Order ordering a congressional vacancy election in the 
Fourth Congressional District of Colorado to be held on June 25, 2024.   
 
Background and Purpose 
The Honorable Ken Buck, the duly-elected representative of the Fourth Congressional 
District of the State of Colorado to the House of Representatives of the United States Congress, 
has tendered his resignation from office, effective as of March 22, 2024. As his current term ends 
on January 3, 2025, his resignation creates a vacancy in the office of the representative of the 
Fourth Congressional District of Colorado. 
The United States Constitution provides that when a vacancy occurs in the representation 
from any state, the executive authority shall issue a writ of election to fill such vacancy. U.S. 
Const. Article I, Section 2, Clause 4. Pursuant to the United States Constitution, the Colorado 
Constitution, and the Colorado Revised Statutes, authority is vested in the Governor to call a 
congressional vacancy election to fill the vacancy in the office of representative to the United 
States Congress and to set a day to hold an election to fill the vacancy. As Colorado law requires 
the date of the congressional vacancy election to be set no less than 85 days and no more than 
100 days after the date the vacancy occurs, I am ordering that the congressional vacancy election 
shall be held on the already scheduled state primary election day of June 25, 2024. This also 
avoids costly duplication and facilitates maximum voter participation. 

Executive Order D 2024 005 
March 23, 2024 
Page 2 of 2 
 
Declarations and Directives 
I order a congressional vacancy election to be held on June 25, 2024, at which time the 
voters of the Fourth Congressional District of Colorado shall elect a representative to the House 
of Representatives of the United States Congress to fill the unexpired term of Representative Ken 
Buck. Such election shall be conducted pursuant to the laws of the State of Colorado. 
GIVEN under my hand and the 
Executive Seal of the State of 
Colorado this twenty-third  
day of March, 2024. 
Jared Polis 
Governor

title: D 2024 006
full_text:
Executive Order D 2024 006 
May 22, 2024 
Page 1 of 3 
D 2024 006 
EXECUTIVE ORDER 
Declaring a Disaster Emergency Due to the U.S. 50 Blue Mesa Middle Bridge Closure in 
Gunnison, Hinsdale, and Montrose Counties, and Authorizing Employment of the 
Colorado National Guard 
Pursuant to the authority vested in the Governor of the State of Colorado and, in 
particular, pursuant to Article IV, Section 2, of the Colorado Constitution and the relevant 
portions of the Colorado Disaster Emergency Act, C.R.S. § 24-33.5-701, et seq., I, Jared Polis, 
Governor of the State of Colorado, issue this Executive Order declaring a state of disaster 
emergency due to the U.S. 50 Blue Mesa Middle Bridge closure in Gunnison, Hinsdale, and 
Montrose Counties, enabling State and federal agencies to coordinate response, consequence 
management, mitigation, and recovery efforts.  
Further, pursuant to Article IV, Section 5 of the Colorado Constitution and C.R.S. § 28-3-
104, I authorize employing unarmed members of the Colorado National Guard for the purpose of 
responding to the U.S. 50 Blue Mesa Middle Bridge closure and providing support, assisting law 
enforcement agencies with traffic control, and supporting incident management. 
 
Background and Purpose 
The Governor is responsible for meeting the dangers to the State and people presented by 
disasters. C.R.S. § 24-33.5-704(1). The Colorado Disaster Emergency Act defines a disaster as 
“the occurrence or imminent threat of widespread or severe damages, injury or loss of life or 
property resulting from any natural cause or cause of human origin.” C.R.S. § 24-33.5-703(3). 
On April 17, 2024, Colorado Department of Transportation (CDOT) engineers, while 
performing federally required bridge inspections specific to bridges comprised of T-1 steel (a 
material used in the 1950s and 1960s), identified a crack in the U.S. 50 Blue Mesa Middle 
Bridge (Blue Mesa Bridge). CDOT engineers reduced the use of the Blue Mesa Bridge to one 
lane of traffic and continued inspections.  
On April 18, 2024, upon further inspection and non-destructive testing of the Blue Mesa 
Bridge, Federal Highway Administration (FHWA) and CDOT engineers determined that the 
visual surface crack and internal defects within the butt weld of the Blue Mesa Bridge were 
critical findings that required closure of the Blue Mesa Bridge in order to protect the safety of the 
traveling public. The engineers immediately closed the Blue Mesa Bridge to traffic.  

Executive Order D 2024 006 
May 22, 2024 
Page 2 of 3 
The Blue Mesa Bridge provides a necessary connection to utilize U.S. 50, the main 
transportation route in the region. The closure of the Blue Mesa Bridge creates significant strains 
on the local economy, health and medical services including emergency care, education, 
agricultural activities, and daily travel to and from work for community members. While two 
alternate state routes have been identified to bypass the Blue Mesa Bridge closure, both routes 
create five to six-hour commutes for community members and businesses. In addition, the use of 
these alternate routes is likely to have an adverse impact on the roadway systems in surrounding 
towns, cities, and counties which are not accustomed to a high volume of traffic. As part of the 
disaster emergency, the State is investing in maintaining county routes to help them absorb more 
traffic. 
On April 23, 2024, at approximately 3:13 PM, the Lieutenant Governor, acting as 
Governor, verbally declared a state of disaster emergency due to the Blue Mesa Bridge closure in 
Gunnison, Hinsdale, and Montrose Counties, and activated the State of Colorado Emergency 
Operations Plan. The Lieutenant Governor also verbally authorized employment and use of the 
Colorado National Guard in an unarmed capacity for the purpose of responding to the Blue Mesa 
Bridge closure and providing support, assisting law enforcement agencies with traffic control, 
and supporting incident management.  
 
Declarations and Directives 
A. 
The U.S. 50 Blue Mesa Middle Bridge closure and associated impacts in 
Gunnison, Hinsdale, and Montrose Counties constitute a disaster emergency 
under C.R.S. § 24-33.5-701, et seq. The Lieutenant Governor’s verbal order of 
April 23, 2024, declaring a disaster emergency, is hereby memorialized by this 
Executive Order and shall have the full force and effect of law as if it were 
contained in this Executive Order. 
B. 
Further, pursuant to C.R.S. § 28-3-104, the Lieutenant Governor’s verbal order of 
April 23, 2024, authorizing employment and use of the Colorado National Guard 
in an unarmed capacity for the purpose of responding to the Blue Mesa Bridge 
closure and providing support, assisting law enforcement agencies with traffic 
control, and supporting incident management, is hereby memorialized by this 
Executive Order and shall have the full force and effect of law as if it were 
contained in this Executive Order. 
C. 
The Lieutenant Governor’s verbal order of April 23, 2024, activated the State 
Emergency Operations Plan. All State departments and agencies were authorized 
to take whatever actions may be required and requested by the Director or their 
delegate of the Office of Emergency Management, pursuant to the State 
Emergency Operations Plan, including provision of appropriate staff and 
equipment as necessary. 

Executive Order D 2024 006 
May 22, 2024 
Page 3 of 3 
D. 
Pursuant to C.R.S. § 24-33.5-706(4)(b), funds in the Disaster Emergency Fund 
(DEF) are insufficient to pay for response, consequence management, mitigation, 
and recovery efforts related to the Blue Mesa Bridge closure. Therefore, I order 
that five million dollars ($5,000,000) be transferred from the State Emergency 
Reserve Cash Fund (SERCF) to the DEF. I encumber five million dollars 
($5,000,000) in the DEF to pay for response, consequence management, 
mitigation, and recovery efforts related to the Blue Mesa Bridge closure. 
E. 
I direct State agencies to pursue all available federal funding for response and 
recovery operations, including but not limited to the Federal Highway 
Administration, U.S. Department of Transportation, and U.S. Department of 
Agriculture. 
F. 
Pursuant to C.R.S. § 24-33.5-705.4(5), the Director of the Office of Emergency 
Management is hereby authorized and directed to allocate the funding described 
in Section II.D to the appropriate government agencies and non-profit 
organizations and execute awards, purchase orders, or other mechanisms to effect 
the allocation of funds. The funds described in Section II.D shall remain available 
for this purpose for seventy-two (72) months from the date of this Executive 
Order, and any unexpended funds shall remain in the DEF. 
 
 
Duration 
This Executive Order shall expire thirty (30) days from April 23, 2024, unless extended 
further by Executive Order, except that the funds described in Section II.D of this Executive 
Order shall remain available for the described purposes for seventy-two (72) months from the 
date of this Executive Order. 
GIVEN under my hand and the 
Executive Seal of the State of 
Colorado this twenty-second  
day of May, 2024. 
 
 
 
 
 
Jared Polis 
Governor

title: D 2024 007
full_text:
Executive Order D 2024 007 
June 10, 2024 
Page 1 of 2 
D 2024 007 
EXECUTIVE ORDER 
Activating the Colorado National Guard to Assist with Election Cybersecurity Defense 
Efforts 
Pursuant to the authority vested in the Governor of the State of Colorado and, in 
particular, pursuant to Article IV, Section 5 of the Colorado Constitution and C.R.S. § 28-3-104, 
et seq., I, Jared Polis, Governor of the State of Colorado, hereby issue this Executive Order 
activating no less than four (4) and up to six (6) unarmed members of the Colorado National 
Guard Defensive Cyber Operations Element to assist with election cybersecurity defense efforts 
during the 2024 Primary Election.  
 
Background and Purpose 
Like many states, Colorado uses online voter registration systems that allow eligible 
citizens to access and update their voter registration information. These systems are protected by 
various security features that maintain confidentiality and prevent data tampering or interference 
with data. 
Even with such security features, online voter registration systems could provide an 
avenue for cyber actors to gain unlawful access to voter registration databases. Outside of 
Colorado, voter registration databases have been compromised by cyber actors hacking into 
various systems. While cyber actors are unable to modify voter records, breaches could result in 
the release of voters’ personally identifiable information. The exposure of voters’ personally 
identifiable information does not threaten the integrity of our State elections, but it could 
undermine public confidence in the system and suppress voter registration. The Colorado 
National Guard Defensive Cyber Operations Element has unique cyber capabilities to assist in 
protecting critical election infrastructure. 
The Colorado Secretary of State has requested that the Colorado National Guard 
Defensive Cyber Operations Element provide election cybersecurity support on June 18, 2024, 
and extending for up to thirty (30) days, for the 2024 Primary Election. By activating no less 
than four (4) and up to six (6) unarmed members of the Colorado National Guard Defensive 
Cyber Operations Element, the National Guard will support the Secretary of State with election 
cyber defense efforts up to and including the 2024 Primary Election on June 25, 2024. 
 

Executive Order D 2024 007 
June 10, 2024 
Page 2 of 2 
 
 
Declarations and Directives 
On June 4, 2024, the Colorado Secretary of State requested support from the Colorado 
National Guard Defensive Cyber Operations Element for the 2024 Primary Election occurring on 
June 25, 2024. Pursuant to C.R.S. § 28-3-104, the Governor may employ the National Guard for 
the defense of the State. I hereby activate no less than four (4) and up to six (6) unarmed 
members of the Colorado National Guard Defensive Cyber Operations Element to participate in 
training activities and assist with election cybersecurity defense efforts on June 18, 2024, and 
extending for up to thirty (30) days, for the 2024 Primary Election. 
 
Duration 
This Executive Order shall expire thirty (30) days from June 18, 2024, unless extended 
further by Executive Order. 
GIVEN under my hand and the 
Executive Seal of the State of 
Colorado this tenth  
day of June, 2024. 
Jared Polis 
Governor

title: D 2024 008
full_text:
Executive Order D 2024 008 
August 2, 2024 
Page 1 of 4 
D 2024 008 
EXECUTIVE ORDER 
Declaring and Extending a Disaster Emergency Due to Highly Pathogenic Avian Influenza 
in Weld County 
Pursuant to the authority vested in the Governor of the State of Colorado and, in 
particular, pursuant to Article IV, Section 2 of the Colorado Constitution and the relevant 
portions of the Colorado Disaster Emergency Act, C.R.S. § 24-33.5-701, et seq., I, Jared Polis, 
Governor of the State of Colorado, hereby issue this Executive Order declaring and extending a 
state of disaster emergency due to the Highly Pathogenic Avian Influenza in Weld County, 
enabling State agencies to better support our agriculture sector by coordinating for statewide 
monitoring and mitigation of disease spread, response, consequence management, and recovery 
efforts, and enabling statewide surveillance and additional statewide protective measures for 
Grade A dairy operations.  
 
Background and Purpose 
The Governor is responsible for meeting the dangers to the State and people presented by 
disasters. C.R.S. § 24-33.5-704(1). The Colorado Disaster Emergency Act defines a disaster as 
“the occurrence or imminent threat of widespread or severe damage, injury, or loss of life or 
property resulting from any natural cause or cause of human origin.” C.R.S § 24-33.5-703(3). 
Highly Pathogenic Avian Influenza (HPAI) was initially detected in the United States in 
the wild bird population in January 2022 in South Carolina and in the domestic poultry 
population in February 2022 in Indiana. HPAI has spread across the United States over the past 
2.5 years. The disease spreads rapidly among wild and domestic poultry. It has a mortality rate 
above ninety percent in poultry.  
HPAI was detected in wild birds in Colorado on March 24, 2022, with ongoing detection 
in the State since that time. Cases in Colorado are occurring in wild, domestic, and commercial 
flocks, including occasional spillover into mammals. 
In March 2024, the USDA confirmed the detection of HPAI (H5N1 avian flu virus) in 
dairy cattle in the Texas Panhandle region. Since then, the virus has spread to 12 states, 
impacting more than 163 dairies. The spread of this virus has been via cow-to-cow and farm-to-
farm transmission. Unlike in previous outbreaks, the virus is not currently being spread by wild 
birds. Colorado has the highest number of reported cases of HPAI in dairy herds in the United 
States, with 50 confirmed detections.  

Executive Order D 2024 008 
August 2, 2024 
Page 2 of 4 
 
On July 5, 2024, HPAI was confirmed in a commercial table egg layer facility in Weld 
County, affecting 1.8 million birds. On July 15, 2024, HPAI was confirmed in a second 
commercial egg layer facility in Weld County, affecting approximately 1.4 million birds. A third 
commercial facility in Weld County was confirmed on July 19, 2024, impacting approximately 
300,000 birds. The same day, five human HPAI infections in workers at the site were also 
confirmed by the Colorado Department of Public Health & Environment. As of July 23, 2024, a 
total of 10 people have confirmed infections associated with the current outbreak in Weld 
County. 
Through genomic sequencing, it was determined that the strain of the virus in the poultry 
facilities is the same as that found in the affected dairies. This suggests that infected dairy cattle 
may be a significant source of the virus infecting poultry. 
Oversight of the response to the outbreak in livestock and implementation of statewide 
surveillance and protective measures requires the deployment and coordination of multiple State 
agencies, including the Colorado Department of Agriculture, Colorado Department of Public 
Health & Environment, Colorado Division of Homeland Security and Emergency Management, 
and federal partners including the United States Department of Agriculture and Centers for 
Disease Control and Prevention. 
On July 5, 2024, at approximately 7:00 PM, I verbally declared a disaster emergency for 
the Highly Pathogenic Avian Influenza in Weld County and activated the State Emergency 
Operations Plan. This Executive Order memorializes that verbal declaration and extends the 
declaration of a disaster emergency for an additional thirty (30) days due to the ongoing need to 
identify HPAI cases and due to the potential for further response to affected facilities. 
 
Declarations and Directives 
A. 
HPAI in Weld County and associated statewide impacts constitute a disaster 
emergency under C.R.S § 24-33.5-701, et seq. My verbal order of July 5, 2024, 
declaring a disaster emergency is hereby memorialized by this Executive Order 
and shall have the full force and effect of law as if it were contained within this 
Executive Order. 
B. 
The State Emergency Operations Plan is hereby activated. All State departments 
and agencies shall take whatever actions may be required and requested by the 
Director or Acting Director of the Office of Emergency Management, pursuant to 
the Emergency Support Function 5 Annex of the State Emergency Operations  
 
 

Executive Order D 2024 008 
August 2, 2024 
Page 3 of 4 
 
Plan, and the Food and Agriculture Incident Annex of the State Emergency 
Operations Plan, including provision of appropriate staff and equipment as 
necessary. 
C. 
Personal Protective Equipment at the Office of Emergency Management’s 
Resource Asset Management site shall be made available to protect the health and 
safety of response workers and support personnel at the affected sites, and to 
minimize further spread of HPAI. 
D. 
I authorize expanded statewide testing and surveillance of Grade A dairy facilities 
to minimize and contain the spread of HPAI. I authorize oversight of response 
activities, including depopulation, for impacted commercial poultry locations in 
Weld County. I also authorize HPAI testing and treatment for human workers at 
response sites. 
E. 
Funds in the Disaster Emergency Fund (DEF) are hereby found insufficient to pay 
for statewide monitoring and mitigation of disease spread, response, consequence 
management, and recovery efforts related to HPAI. Therefore, I order that three 
million five hundred thousand dollars ($3,500,000) be transferred from the State 
Emergency Reserve Cash Fund (SERCF) to the DEF. I encumber three million 
five hundred thousand dollars ($3,500,000) in the DEF, established in 
C.R.S. § 24-33.5-706(2)(a), for costs associated with the State’s statewide 
monitoring and mitigation of disease spread, response, consequence management, 
and recovery efforts related to the Highly Pathogenic Avian Influenza in Colorado 
since July 5, 2024. 
F. 
Pursuant to C.R.S. § 24-33.5-705.4(5), the Director of the Office of Emergency 
Management is hereby authorized and directed to allocate the funding to the 
appropriate government agencies and non-profit organizations and execute 
awards, purchase orders, or other mechanisms to effect the allocation of the funds. 
The funds described in Section II.E above shall remain available for this purpose 
until seventy-two (72) months from the date of this Executive Order, and any 
unexpended funds shall remain in the Disaster Emergency Fund. 
G. 
The Office of Emergency Management, in coordination with the Colorado 
Department of Agriculture and the Colorado Department of Public Health & 
Environment, is authorized to coordinate application for any funds available 
related to this disaster emergency. Specifically, the Colorado Department of 
Agriculture is directed to seek reimbursement, including reimbursement from the 
United States Department of Agriculture for response activities under the 2024  

Executive Order D 2024 008 
August 2, 2024 
Page 4 of 4 
 
Highly Pathogenic Avian Influenza Emergency Cooperative Agreement, and 
make payments as needed to agencies providing assistance on this incident. 
 
Duration 
This Executive Order, declaring and extending the disaster emergency, shall expire thirty 
(30) days from August 3, 2024, unless extended further by Executive Order, except that the 
funds described in Section II.E above shall remain available for the described purposes for 
seventy-two (72) months from the date of this Executive Order. 
GIVEN under my hand and the 
Executive Seal of the State of 
Colorado this second  
day of August, 2024. 
Jared Polis 
Governor

title: D 2024 009
full_text:
Executive Order D 2024 009 
August 15, 2024 
Page 1 of 2 
D 2024 009 
EXECUTIVE ORDER 
Call for the Second Extraordinary Session of the Seventy-Fourth General Assembly 
Pursuant to the authority vested in the Governor of the State of Colorado and, in 
particular, pursuant to Article IV, Section 9 of the Colorado Constitution, and as recognized in 
Article V, Section 7, I, Jared Polis, Governor of the State of Colorado, hereby find that the 
following extraordinary occasions exist to convene the Seventy-Fourth General Assembly to 
meet in special session. 
 
Background 
Property taxes in Colorado are used to support many important causes including public 
schools, county and municipal governments, higher education institutions, and special districts, 
which include fire protection, ambulance, park and recreation, and water and sanitation districts. 
Property taxes also indirectly support our State budget by providing funding to schools which the 
State is otherwise required to fund.  
At the same time, because of a record increase in property values, property taxes continue 
to consume an ever increasing percentage of a family’s income. Some Coloradans have 
experienced property tax increases exceeding 30%.  
The Colorado General Assembly has made significant efforts to reduce property taxes in 
a sustainable and responsible way. Senate Bill 22-238, passed with bipartisan support, reduced 
property tax assessment rates for 2023 and 2024, which reduced assessment valuation from 
7.15% to 6.765% of actual value for residential property and 29% to 27.9% of actual value for 
nonresidential property. During the 2023 special session, the General Assembly, again 
demonstrating bipartisan effort, further reduced residential property tax rates for 2023 to 6.7% of 
actual value and increased the amount that is exempt from taxation from $15,000 to $55,000. 
And in the 2024 legislative session, the General Assembly passed Senate Bill 24-233 with 
bipartisan support and reduced property taxes by $1.3 billion for 2024 and 2025, providing an 
average of $500 in relief for Colorado families, while at the same time prioritizing school 
funding revenue.  
I signed each of these bills to deliver economic relief to hard working Coloradans.  
I am calling this extraordinary session to ask the Colorado General Assembly to once 
again deliver bipartisan property tax relief to help combat the urgent impact of rising property  

Executive Order D 2024 009 
August 15, 2024 
Page 2 of 2 
 
taxes, while protecting revenue for schools and local governments. I am hopeful and optimistic 
that a compromise can be reached to end the property tax wars and avoid any current or future 
ballot measures on the subject for the foreseeable future. 
This call for an extraordinary session of the General Assembly does not prescribe the 
specific form that the legislation should take; however, as required by Article IV, Section 9 of 
the Colorado Constitution, the business to be transacted at this special session shall be limited to 
the matters stated in Section III of this Executive Order. 
 
Purpose and Need 
Coloradans continue to be burdened by increasing property taxes and need reliability in 
the amounts they will pay in future years. I am encouraging the General Assembly to take up 
legislation that honors the work of legislators and stakeholders over the past weeks. The General 
Assembly should consider taking legislative action to maintain and lower future tax rates, adopt 
a tighter property tax cap to further limit how quickly property tax revenue can increase, and 
implement a cap on property taxes paid to school districts that considers inflation and student 
growth. 
 
Proclamation 
I, Jared Polis, Governor of the State of Colorado, with this proclamation find 
extraordinary occasions exist to convene the Seventy-Fourth General Assembly of this State and 
summon the members of the Seventy-Fourth General Assembly to meet in Special Session at 
10:00 AM on August 26, 2024, at the State Capitol, in the City and County of Denver, and 
designate the following specific subjects for consideration, appropriate legislative action, and 
funding:  
Concerning property taxes starting with the property tax year 
commencing on January 1, 2025. 
GIVEN under my hand and the 
Executive Seal of the State of 
Colorado this fifteenth  
day of August, 2024. 
 
 
 
Jared Polis 
Governor

title: D 2024 010
full_text:
Executive Order D 2024 010 
August 28, 2024 
Page 1 of 3 
D 2024 010 
EXECUTIVE ORDER 
Declaring a Disaster Emergency Due to the Currant Creek Fire in Delta County and 
Authorizing Employment of the Colorado National Guard 
Pursuant to the authority vested in the Governor of the State of Colorado and, in 
particular, pursuant to Article IV, Section 2, of the Colorado Constitution and the relevant 
portions of the Colorado Disaster Emergency Act, C.R.S. § 24-33.5-701, et seq., I, Jared Polis, 
Governor of the State of Colorado, issue this Executive Order declaring a state of disaster 
emergency due to the Currant Creek Fire in Delta County, enabling State and Federal agencies to 
coordinate fire suppression, response, consequence management, mitigation, and recovery 
efforts. 
Further, pursuant to Article IV, Section 5 of the Colorado Constitution and C.R.S. § 28-3-
104, I authorize employing unarmed members of the Colorado National Guard for the purpose of 
responding to the Currant Creek Fire by supporting fire suppression efforts, including with the 
use of aviation assets if needed, and assisting law enforcement agencies with traffic control, 
security needs, and supporting incident management. 
 
Background and Purpose 
The Governor is responsible for meeting the dangers to the State and people presented by 
disasters. C.R.S. § 24-33.5-704(1). The Colorado Disaster Emergency Act defines a disaster as 
“the occurrence or imminent threat of widespread or severe damage, injury or loss of life or 
property resulting from any natural cause or cause of human origin including but not limited to 
fire.” C.R.S. § 24-33.5-703(3). 
On July 27, 2024, the Currant Creek Fire started near Cedaredge, Colorado, in Delta 
County. The Currant Creek Fire burned 188 acres on Bureau of Land Management and private 
land in rough and challenging terrain in high temperatures resulting in heat advisories. Within a 
five mile radius, the fire threatened 518 homes, a watershed for the town of Hotchkiss, and one 
communication tower, exceeding local capacity to manage the fire. Three houses were placed on 
evacuation order and 200 houses were put on pre-evacuation status. 
On July 30, 2024, at approximately 3:15pm, I verbally declared a disaster emergency due 
to the Currant Creek Fire in Delta County and activated the State of Colorado Emergency 
Operations Plan. On July 31, 2024, I verbally authorized employing unarmed members of the 
Colorado National Guard for the purpose of responding to the Currant Creek Fire by supporting  

Executive Order D 2024 010 
August 28, 2024 
Page 2 of 3 
 
fire suppression efforts, including with the use of aviation assets if needed, and assisting law 
enforcement agencies with traffic control, security needs, and supporting incident management. 
On August 2, 2024, the fire was 56% contained. As of August 5, 2024, the fire was 100% 
contained. 
 
Declarations and Directives 
A. 
The Currant Creek Fire and associated impacts constitute a disaster emergency 
under C.R.S. § 24-33.5-701, et seq. My verbal order of July 30, 2024, declaring a 
disaster emergency, is hereby memorialized by this Executive Order and shall 
have the full force and effect of law as if it were contained within this Executive 
Order 
B. 
Further, pursuant to C.R.S. § 28-3-104, my verbal order of July 31, 2024, 
authorizing employing unarmed members of the Colorado National Guard for the 
purpose of responding to the Currant Creek Fire, is hereby memorialized by this 
Executive Order and shall have the full force and effect of law as if it were 
contained in this Executive Order. 
C. 
My verbal order of July 30, 2024, activated the State Emergency Operations Plan. 
All State departments and agencies are authorized to take whatever actions may 
be required and requested by the Director or Acting Director of the Office of 
Emergency Management pursuant to the State Emergency Operations Plan, 
including provision of appropriate staff and equipment, as necessary. 
D. 
I direct State agencies to pursue all available federal funding for response and 
recovery operations, including but not limited to the Department of Homeland 
Security Federal Emergency Management Agency (FEMA). 
E. 
Pursuant to C.R.S. § 24-33.5-706(4)(b), funds in the Disaster Emergency Fund 
(DEF) are insufficient to pay for fire suppression, response, consequence 
management, mitigation, and recovery efforts. Therefore, I order that one hundred 
fifty seven thousand dollars ($157,000) be transferred from the State Emergency 
Reserve Cash Fund (SERCF) to the DEF. I encumber one hundred fifty seven 
thousand dollars ($157,000) in the DEF to pay for fire suppression, response, 
consequence management, mitigation, and recovery efforts related to the Currant 
Creek Fire in Delta County. 
F. 
Pursuant to C.R.S. § 24-33.5-705.4(5), the Director of the Office of Emergency 
Management is hereby authorized and directed to allocate the funding to the 
appropriate government agencies and nonprofit organizations and execute awards,  

Executive Order D 2024 010 
August 28, 2024 
Page 3 of 3 
 
purchase orders or other mechanisms to effect the allocation of funds. The funds 
described in Section II(E) above shall remain available for this purpose until 
seventy-two (72) months from the date of this Executive Order, and any 
unexpended funds shall remain in the DEF. 
 
Duration 
This Executive Order shall expire thirty (30) days from July 30, 2024, unless extended 
further by Executive Order, except that the funds described in Section II(E) above shall remain 
available for the described purposes for seventy-two (72) months from the date of this Executive 
Order. 
GIVEN under my hand and the 
Executive Seal of the State of 
Colorado this twenty-eighth  
day of August, 2024. 
Jared Polis 
Governor

title: D 2024 011
full_text:
Executive Order D 2024 011 
August 28, 2024 
Page 1 of 3 
D 2024 011 
EXECUTIVE ORDER 
Declaring a Disaster Emergency Due to the Alexander Mountain Fire in Larimer County 
and Authorizing Employment of the Colorado National Guard 
Pursuant to the authority vested in the Governor of the State of Colorado and, in 
particular, pursuant to Article IV, Section 2, of the Colorado Constitution and the relevant 
portions of the Colorado Disaster Emergency Act, C.R.S. § 24-33.5-701, et seq., I, Jared Polis, 
Governor of the State of Colorado, issue this Executive Order declaring a state of disaster 
emergency due to the Alexander Mountain Fire in Larimer County, enabling State and Federal 
agencies to coordinate fire suppression, response, consequence management, mitigation, and 
recovery efforts.  
Further, pursuant to Article IV, Section 5 of the Colorado Constitution and pursuant to 
C.R.S. § 28-3-104, I authorize employing unarmed members of the Colorado National Guard for 
the purpose of responding to the Alexander Mountain Fire by supporting fire suppression efforts, 
including with the use of aviation assets if needed, and assisting law enforcement agencies with 
traffic control, security needs, and supporting incident management. 
 
Background and Purpose 
The Governor is responsible for meeting the dangers to the State and people presented by 
disasters. C.R.S. § 24-33.5-704(1). The Colorado Disaster Emergency Act defines a disaster as 
“the occurrence or imminent threat of widespread or severe damage, injury or loss of life or 
property resulting from any natural cause or cause of human origin including but not limited to 
fire.” C.R.S. § 24-33.5-703(3). 
On July 29, 2024, the Alexander Mountain Fire started in Larimer County, seven miles 
west of Loveland, Colorado. The Alexander Mountain Fire burned 950 acres on July 29, 2024, 
and by July 30, 2024, had increased to 5,100 acres, burning on Federal and private land. Within a 
three mile radius of the center of the fire, 318 homes were threatened with approximately 814 
people residing in the area. The communities of Drake and Masonville were placed on 
evacuation order. Multiple radio and cell towers were threatened and the local capacity was 
exceeded. 
On July 30, 2024, at approximately 3:15pm, I verbally declared a disaster emergency due 
to the Alexander Mountain Fire in Larimer County, and activated the State of Colorado 
Emergency Operations Plan. On July 31, 2024, I verbally authorized employing unarmed  

Executive Order D 2024 011 
August 28, 2024 
Page 2 of 3 
 
members of the Colorado National Guard for the purpose of responding to the Alexander 
Mountain Fire by supporting fire suppression efforts, including with the use of aviation assets if 
needed, and assisting law enforcement agencies with traffic control, security needs, and 
supporting incident management.   
The availability of wildland fire resources was extremely limited due to the occurrence of 
wildfires across the western United States resulting in a heightened level of national 
preparedness. On July 31, 2024, the Federal Emergency Management Agency (FEMA) declared 
the fire eligible for a Fire Management Assistance Grant (FMAG). On August 2, 2024, the fire 
had burned 9,053 acres and was 5% contained. By August 7, 2024 the fire had burned 9,668 
acres and was 83% contained. As of August 17, 2024, the Alexander Mountain Fire was 100% 
contained. 
 
Declarations and Directives 
A. 
The Alexander Mountain Fire and associated impacts constitute a disaster 
emergency under C.R.S. § 24-33.5-701, et seq. My verbal order of July 30, 2024, 
declaring a disaster emergency is hereby memorialized by this Executive Order 
and shall have the full force and effect of law as if it were contained within this 
Executive Order. 
B. 
Further, pursuant to C.R.S. § 28-3-104, my verbal order of July 31, 2024, 
authorizing employing unarmed members of the Colorado National Guard for the 
purpose of responding to the Alexander Mountain Fire, is hereby memorialized by 
this Executive Order and shall have the full force and effect of law as if it were 
contained within this Executive Order. 
C. 
My verbal order on July 30, 2024 activated the State Emergency Operations Plan. 
All State departments and agencies shall take whatever actions required and 
requested by the Director or Acting Director of the Office of Emergency 
Management pursuant to the State Emergency Operations Plan, including 
provision of appropriate staff and equipment, as necessary. 
D. 
I direct State agencies to pursue all available federal funding for response and 
recovery operations, including but not limited to the Department of Homeland 
Security Federal Emergency Management Agency (FEMA). 
E. 
Pursuant to C.R.S. § 24-33.5-706(4)(b), funds in the Disaster Emergency Fund 
(DEF) are insufficient to pay for fire suppression, response, consequence 
management, mitigation, and recovery efforts. Therefore, I order that five million 
four hundred forty five thousand dollars ($5,445,000) be transferred from the  

Executive Order D 2024 011 
August 28, 2024 
Page 3 of 3 
 
State Emergency Reserve Cash Fund (SERCF) to the DEF. I encumber five 
million four hundred forty five thousand dollars ($5,445,000) in the DEF to pay 
for fire suppression, response, consequence management, mitigation, and 
recovery efforts related to the Alexander Mountain Fire in Larimer County. 
F. 
Pursuant to C.R.S. § 24-33.5-705.4(5), the Director of the Office of Emergency 
Management is hereby authorized and directed to allocate the funding to the 
appropriate government agencies and nonprofit organizations and execute awards, 
purchase orders or other mechanisms to effect the allocation of funds. The funds 
described in Section II(E) above shall remain available for this purpose until 
seventy-two (72) months from the date of this Executive Order, and any 
unexpended funds shall remain in the DEF. 
 
Duration 
This Executive Order shall expire thirty (30) days from July 30, 2024, unless extended 
further by Executive Order, except that the funds described in Section II(E) above shall remain 
available for the described purposes for seventy-two (72) months from the date of this Executive 
Order. 
GIVEN under my hand and the 
Executive Seal of the State of 
Colorado this twenty-eighth  
day of August, 2024. 
Jared Polis 
Governor

title: D 2024 012
full_text:
Executive Order D 2024 012 
August 28, 2024 
Page 1 of 3 
D 2024 012 
EXECUTIVE ORDER 
Declaring a Disaster Emergency Due to the Stone Mountain Fire in Boulder and Larimer 
Counties and Authorizing Employment of the Colorado National Guard 
Pursuant to the authority vested in the Governor of the State of Colorado and, in 
particular, pursuant to Article IV, Section 2, of the Colorado Constitution and the relevant 
portions of the Colorado Disaster Emergency Act, C.R.S. § 24-33.5-701, et seq., I, Jared Polis, 
Governor of the State of Colorado, issue this Executive Order declaring a state of disaster 
emergency due to the Stone Mountain Fire in Boulder and Larimer Counties, enabling State and 
Federal agencies to coordinate fire suppression, response, consequence management, mitigation, 
and recovery efforts.  
Further, pursuant to Article IV, Section 5 of the Colorado Constitution and C.R.S. § 28-3-
104, I authorize employing unarmed members of the Colorado National Guard for the purpose of 
responding to the Stone Mountain Fire by supporting fire suppression efforts, including with the 
use of aviation assets if needed, and assisting law enforcement agencies with traffic control, 
security needs, and supporting incident management. 
 
Background and Purpose 
The Governor is responsible for meeting the dangers to the State and people presented by 
disasters. C.R.S. § 24-33.5-704(1). The Colorado Disaster Emergency Act defines a disaster as 
“the occurrence or imminent threat of widespread or severe damage, injury, or loss of life or 
property resulting from any natural cause or cause of human origin, including but not limited to 
fire.” C.R.S. § 24-33.5-703(3).  
On July 30, 2024, the Stone Mountain Fire started in the town of Lyons, located in 
Boulder County. Boulder County issued mandatory and voluntary evacuations, and up to 500 
structures were threatened by fire. Five residences were destroyed, multiple residences were 
damaged, and multiple outbuildings were destroyed and damaged. Tragically, one life was lost 
due to the Stone Mountain Fire. 
On July 30, 2024, at approximately 6:35pm, I verbally declared a disaster emergency due 
to the Stone Mountain Fire in Boulder County, and activated the State of Colorado Emergency 
Operations Plan. On July 31, 2024, I verbally authorized employing unarmed members of the 
Colorado National Guard for the purpose of responding to the Stone Mountain Fire by 
supporting fire suppression efforts, including with the use of aviation assets if needed, and  

Executive Order D 2024 012 
August 28, 2024 
Page 2 of 3 
 
assisting law enforcement agencies with traffic control, security needs, and supporting incident 
management.  
On July 31, 2024, the Stone Mountain Fire had burned 1,319 acres and burned into 
Larimer County. The Federal Emergency Management Agency (FEMA) declared the Stone 
Mountain Fire eligible for a Fire Management Assistance Grant (FMAG). By August 1, 2024, 
the fire had burned 1,553 acres on private land, and was 30% contained. As of August 4, 2024, 
the Stone Mountain Fire was 100% contained. This Executive Order expands my disaster 
declaration to include both Boulder and Larimer Counties. 
 
Declarations and Directives 
A. 
The Stone Mountain Fire and associated impacts constitute a disaster emergency 
under C.R.S. § 24-33.5-701, et seq. My verbal order of July 30, 2024, declaring a 
disaster emergency, is hereby memorialized by this Executive Order and shall 
have the full force and effect of law as if it were contained within this Executive 
Order. This Executive Order expands my disaster declaration to include both 
Boulder and Larimer Counties. 
B. 
Further, pursuant to C.R.S. § 28-3-104, my verbal order of July 31, 2024, 
authorizing employing unarmed members of the Colorado National Guard for the 
purpose of responding to the Stone Mountain Fire, is hereby memorialized by this 
Executive Order and shall have the full force and effect of law as if it were 
contained within this Executive Order. 
C. 
My verbal order of July 30, 2024, activated the State Emergency Operations Plan. 
All State departments and agencies are authorized to take whatever actions may 
be required and requested by the Director or Acting Director of the Office of 
Emergency Management pursuant to the State Emergency Operations Plan, 
including provision of appropriate staff and equipment, as necessary. 
D. 
I direct State agencies to pursue all available federal funding for response and 
recovery operations, including but not limited to the Department of Homeland 
Security Federal Emergency Management Agency (FEMA). 
E. 
Pursuant to C.R.S. § 24-33.5-706(4)(b), funds in the Disaster Emergency Fund 
(DEF) are insufficient to pay for fire suppression, response, consequence 
management, mitigation, and recovery efforts. Therefore, I order that two million 
nine hundred sixty thousand dollars ($2,960,000) be transferred from the State 
Emergency Reserve Cash Fund (SERCF) to the DEF. I encumber two million 
nine hundred sixty thousand dollars ($2,960,000) in the DEF to pay for fire  

Executive Order D 2024 012 
August 28, 2024 
Page 3 of 3 
 
suppression, response, consequence management, mitigation, and recovery efforts 
related to the Stone Mountain Fire in Boulder and Larimer Counties. 
F. 
Pursuant to C.R.S. § 24-33.5-705.4(5), the Director of the Office of Emergency 
Management is hereby authorized and directed to allocate the funding to the 
appropriate government agencies and nonprofit organizations and execute awards, 
purchase orders or other mechanisms to effect the allocation of funds. The funds 
described in Section II(E) above shall remain available for this purpose until 
seventy-two (72) months from the date of this Executive Order, and any 
unexpended funds shall remain in the DEF. 
 
Duration 
This Executive Order shall expire thirty (30) days from July 30, 2024, unless extended 
further by Executive Order, except that the funds described in Section II(E) above shall remain 
available for the described purposes for seventy-two (72) months from the date of this Executive 
Order. 
GIVEN under my hand and the 
Executive Seal of the State of 
Colorado this twenty-eighth  
day of August, 2024. 
Jared Polis 
Governor

title: D 2024 013
full_text:
Executive Order D 2024 013 
August 28, 2024 
Page 1 of 3 
D 2024 013 
EXECUTIVE ORDER 
Declaring a Disaster Emergency Due to the Quarry Fire in Jefferson County and 
Authorizing Employment of the Colorado National Guard 
Pursuant to the authority vested in the Governor of the State of Colorado and, in 
particular, pursuant to Article IV, Section 2, of the Colorado Constitution and the relevant 
portions of the Colorado Disaster Emergency Act, C.R.S. § 24-33.5-701, et seq., I, Jared Polis, 
Governor of the State of Colorado, issue this Executive Order declaring a state of disaster 
emergency due to the Quarry Fire in Jefferson County, enabling State and Federal agencies to 
coordinate fire suppression response, consequence management, mitigation, and recovery efforts.  
Further, pursuant to Article IV, Section 5 of the Colorado Constitution and C.R.S. § 28-3-
104, I authorize employing unarmed members of the Colorado National Guard for the purpose of 
responding to the Quarry Fire by supporting fire suppression efforts, including with the use of 
aviation assets if needed, and assisting law enforcement agencies with traffic control, security 
needs, and supporting incident management. 
 
Background and Purpose 
The Governor is responsible for meeting the dangers to the State and people presented by 
disasters. C.R.S. § 24-33.5-704(1). The Colorado Disaster Emergency Act defines a disaster as 
“the occurrence or imminent threat of widespread or severe damage, injury or loss of life or 
property resulting from any natural cause or cause of human origin including but not limited to 
fire.” C.R.S. § 24-33.5-703(3). 
On July 30, 2024, the Quarry Fire started in Jefferson County, six miles west of Littleton, 
Colorado. Within a two mile radius of the fire center, there were 771 buildings, 15 
communication towers, 2 fire stations and 1 airport, with a population impact of approximately 
243 persons, exceeding local capacity to manage the fire. The topography entailed steep drainage 
and complex terrain on the front range foothills with hot and dry weather. There were 575 homes 
in three subdivisions that were evacuated in response to the fire.  
On July 31, 2024, at approximately 9:45am, I verbally declared a disaster emergency due 
to the Quarry Fire in Jefferson County and activated the State of Colorado Emergency 
Operations Plan. Also on July 31, 2024, I verbally authorized employing unarmed members of 
the Colorado National Guard for the purpose of responding to the Quarry Fire by supporting fire  
 

Executive Order D 2024 013 
August 28, 2024 
Page 2 of 3 
 
suppression efforts, including with the use of aviation assets if needed, and assisting law 
enforcement agencies with traffic control, security needs, and supporting incident management. 
Between July 30 and August 1, 2024, the Quarry Fire burned 341 acres on private land. 
On August 1, 2024, the Federal Emergency Management Agency (FEMA) declared the Quarry 
Fire eligible for a Fire Management Assistant Grant (FMAG). On August 4, 2024, there were 
241 homes still without power. As of August 4, 2024, the fire had burned 578 acres and was 
100% contained. 
 
Declarations and Directives 
A. 
The Quarry Fire and associated impacts constitute a disaster emergency under 
C.R.S. § 24-33.5-701, et seq. My verbal order of July 31, 2024, declaring a 
disaster emergency, is hereby memorialized by this Executive Order, and shall 
have the full force and effect of law as if it were contained within this Executive 
Order. 
B. 
Further, pursuant to C.R.S. § 28-3-104, my verbal order of July 31, 2024, 
authorizing employing unarmed members of the Colorado National Guard for the 
purpose of responding to the Quarry Fire, is hereby memorialized by this 
Executive Order and shall have the full force and effect of law as if it were 
contained within this Executive Order. 
C. 
My verbal order of July 31, 2024, activated the State Emergency Operations Plan. 
All State departments and agencies are authorized to take whatever actions may 
be required and requested by the Director or Acting Director of the Office of 
Emergency Management pursuant to the State Emergency Operations Plan, 
including provision of appropriate staff and equipment, as necessary. 
D. 
I direct State agencies to pursue all available federal funding for response and 
recovery operations, including but not limited to the Department of Homeland 
Security Federal Emergency Management Agency (FEMA). 
E. 
Pursuant to C.R.S. § 24-33.5-706(4)(b), funds in the Disaster Emergency Fund 
(DEF) are insufficient to pay for fire suppression, response, consequence 
management, mitigation, and recovery efforts. Therefore, I order that four million 
eight hundred thirty nine thousand dollars ($4,839,000) be transferred from the 
State Emergency Reserve Cash Fund (SERCF) to the DEF. I encumber four 
million eight hundred thirty nine thousand dollars ($4,839,000) in the DEF to pay 
for fire suppression, response, consequence management, mitigation, and 
recovery efforts related to the Quarry Fire in Jefferson County.  

Executive Order D 2024 013 
August 28, 2024 
Page 3 of 3 
 
 
F. 
Pursuant to C.R.S. § 24-33.5-705.4(5), the Director of the Office of Emergency 
Management is hereby authorized and directed to allocate the funding to the 
appropriate government agencies and nonprofit organizations and execute awards, 
purchase orders or other mechanisms to effect the allocation of funds. The funds 
described in Section II(E) above shall remain available for this purpose until 
seventy-two (72) months from the date of this Executive Order, and any 
unexpended funds shall remain in the DEF. 
 
Duration 
This Executive Order shall expire thirty (30) days from July 31, 2024, unless extended 
further by Executive Order, except that the funds described in Section II(E) above shall remain 
available for the described purposes for seventy-two (72) months from the date of this Executive 
Order. 
GIVEN under my hand and the 
Executive Seal of the State of 
Colorado this twenty-eighth  
day of August, 2024. 
Jared Polis 
Governor

title: D 2024 014
full_text:
Executive Order D 2024 014 
September 20, 2024 
Page 1 of 3 
D 2024 014 
EXECUTIVE ORDER 
Declaring a Disaster Emergency Due to Flooding and Debris Flow in Ouray County 
Pursuant to the authority vested in the Governor of the State of Colorado and, in 
particular, pursuant to Article IV, Section 2 of the Colorado Constitution and the relevant 
portions of the Colorado Disaster Emergency Act, C.R.S. § 24-33.5-701, et seq., I, Jared Polis, 
Governor of the State of Colorado, issue this Executive Order declaring a state of disaster 
emergency due to the Flooding and Debris Flow in Ouray County, enabling State agencies to 
coordinate response, consequence management, mitigation, and recovery efforts.   
 
Background and Purpose 
The Governor is responsible for meeting the dangers to the State and people presented by 
disasters. C.R.S. § 24-33.5-704(1). The Colorado Disaster Emergency Act defines a disaster as 
“the occurrence or imminent threat of widespread or severe damage, injury, or loss of life or 
property resulting from any natural cause or cause of human origin.” C.R.S. § 24-33.5-703(3). 
Beginning August 5, 2024, rain from a monsoonal weather system caused flash flooding, 
landslides, and debris flows in Ouray County. The county issued a local disaster declaration on 
August 13, 2024. The Town of Ridgway and City of Ouray issued local disaster declarations on 
August 14, 2024. 
Numerous roads became damaged and impassable including County Road 17 (CR17), 
County Road 5 (CR5) and County Road 23 (CR23). CR17 is completely closed due to debris 
flow at Corbett Creek. County Roads 5 and 23 were washed out and impassable, but are now 
open.  
The Town of Ridgway experienced the most significant impacts. Debris flows rerouted 
Beaver Creek, destroying the town’s drinking water collection system. A secondary source of 
drinking water is unreliable. Disruption of the secondary source will cause water restrictions. 
Along with Beaver Creek being rerouted, the creek banks have become unstable, with exposed 
tree root systems in many places along the now-widened banks. Roads and a geothermal water 
pipe are also impacted.  

Executive Order D 2024 014 
September 20, 2024 
Page 2 of 3 
On August 23, 2024, at approximately 1:45 PM, I verbally declared a disaster emergency 
due to the Flooding and Debris Flow in Ouray County and activated the State Emergency 
Operations Plan. 
 
Declarations and Directives 
A. 
The Flooding and Debris Flow in Ouray County and associated impacts constitute 
a disaster emergency under C.R.S. § 24-33.5-701, et seq. My verbal order of 
August 23, 2024, declaring a disaster emergency, is hereby memorialized by this 
Executive Order and shall have the full force and effect of law as if it were 
contained within this Executive Order. 
B. 
My verbal order of August 23, 2024, activated the State Emergency Operations 
Plan. All State departments and agencies are authorized to take whatever actions 
may be required and requested by the Director or Acting Director of the Office of 
Emergency Management, pursuant to the State Emergency Operations Plan, 
including provision of appropriate staff and equipment, as necessary. 
C. 
Pursuant to C.R.S. § 24-33.5-706(4)(b), funds in the Disaster Emergency Fund 
(DEF) are insufficient to pay for response, consequence management, mitigation, 
and recovery efforts related to the Flooding and Debris Flow in Ouray County. 
Therefore, I order that four million nine hundred thousand dollars ($4,900,000) be 
transferred from the State Emergency Reserve Cash Fund (SERCF) to the DEF. I 
encumber four million nine hundred thousand dollars ($4,900,000) in the DEF to 
pay for response, consequence management, mitigation, and recovery efforts 
related to the Flooding and Debris Flow in Ouray County.  
D. 
Pursuant to C.R.S. § 24-33.5-705.4(5), the Director of the Office of Emergency 
Management is hereby authorized and directed to allocate the funding to the 
appropriate government agencies and non-profit organizations and execute 
awards, purchase orders, or other mechanisms to effect the allocation of funds. 
The funds described in Section II(C) above shall remain available for this purpose 
until seventy-two (72) months from the date of this Executive Order, and any 
unexpended funds shall remain in the DEF. 

Executive Order D 2024 014 
September 20, 2024 
Page 3 of 3 
 
Duration 
This Executive Order shall expire thirty (30) days from August 23, 2024, unless extended 
further by Executive Order, except that the funds described in Section II(C) above shall remain 
available for the described purposes for seventy-two (72) months from the date of this Executive 
Order. 
GIVEN under my hand and the 
Executive Seal of the State of 
Colorado this twentieth day of September, 
2024. 
Jared Polis 
Governor

title: D 2024 015
full_text:
Executive Order D 2024 015 
October 10, 2024 
Page 1 of 2 
D 2024 015 
EXECUTIVE ORDER 
Activating the Colorado National Guard to Assist with  
Election Cybersecurity Defense Efforts 
Pursuant to the authority vested in the Governor of the State of Colorado and, in 
particular, pursuant to Article IV, Section 5 of the Colorado Constitution and C.R.S. § 28-3-104, 
et seq., I, Jared Polis, Governor of the State of Colorado, hereby issue this Executive Order 
activating no less than four (4) and up to six (6) unarmed members of the Colorado National 
Guard Defensive Cyber Operations Element to assist with election cybersecurity defense efforts 
during the 2024 General Election. 
 
Background and Purpose 
Like many states, Colorado uses online voter registration systems that allow eligible 
citizens to access and update their voter registration information. These systems are protected by 
various security features that maintain confidentiality and prevent data tampering or interference 
with data. 
Even with such security features, online voter registration systems could provide an 
avenue for cyber actors to gain unlawful access to voter registration databases. Outside of 
Colorado, voter registration databases have been compromised by cyber actors hacking into 
various systems. While cyber actors are unable to modify voter records, breaches could result in 
the release of voters’ personally identifiable information. The exposure of voters’ personally 
identifiable information does not threaten the integrity of our State elections, but it could 
undermine public confidence in the system and suppress voter registration. The Colorado 
National Guard Defensive Cyber Operations Element has unique cyber capabilities to assist in 
protecting critical election infrastructure. 
The Colorado Secretary of State has requested that the Colorado National Guard 
Defensive Cyber Operations Element provide election cybersecurity support on October 10, 
2024, and extending for up to thirty (30) days, for the 2024 General Election. By activating no 
less than four (4) and up to six (6) unarmed members of the Colorado National Guard Defensive 
Cyber Operations Element, the National Guard will support the Secretary of State with election 
cyber defense efforts up to and including the 2024 General Election on November 5, 2024. 
 
 

Executive Order D 2024 015 
October 10, 2024 
Page 2 of 2 
 
 
 
Declarations and Directives 
On September 6, 2024, the Colorado Secretary of State requested support from the 
Colorado National Guard Defensive Cyber Operations Element for the 2024 General Election 
occurring on November 5, 2024. Pursuant to C.R.S. § 28-3-104, the Governor may employ the 
National Guard for the defense of the State. I hereby activate no less than four (4) and up to six 
(6) unarmed members of the Colorado National Guard Defensive Cyber Operations Element to 
participate in training activities and assist with election cybersecurity defense efforts on October 
10, 2024, and extending for up to thirty (30) days, for the 2024 General Election. 
 
 
Duration 
This Executive Order shall expire thirty (30) days from October 10, 2024, unless 
extended further by Executive Order. 
GIVEN under my hand and the 
Executive Seal of the State of 
Colorado this tenth  
day of October, 2024. 
Jared Polis 
Governor

title: D 2024 016
